architecture_summary: The paper introduces the Open X-Embodiment Repository, a standardized large-scale robot learning dataset and pretrained checkpoints for X-embodied robot learning. It evaluates RT-1-X and RT-2-X, which adapt RT-1 and RT-2 to co-train on multi-embodiment robotic data with a coarsely aligned action and observation space.

novel_contributions: Open X-Embodiment Repository, an open-source repository which includes large-scale data along with pre-trained model checkpoints for X-embodied robot learning research
Open X-Embodiment Dataset with 1M+ robot trajectories from 22 robot embodiments
RT-X models that train RT-1 and RT-2 on multi-embodiment robotic data to demonstrate positive transfer
A standardized data format for pooling 60 existing robot datasets into a consistent repository

key_claims.claim: The paper assembles a dataset from 22 different robots collected through a collaboration between 21 institutions, demonstrating 527 skills (160266 tasks).
RT-X exhibits positive transfer and improves the capabilities of multiple robots by leveraging experience from other platforms.
The Open X-Embodiment Dataset contains 1M+ real robot trajectories spanning 22 robot embodiments.
RT-1-X outperforms the Original Method trained on each of the robot-specific datasets on 4 of the 5 small-scale dataset domains.
In the large-dataset setting, RT-1-X does not outperform the RT-1 baseline trained on only the embodiment-specific dataset, but the larger RT-2-X model outperforms both the Original Method and RT-1.
RT-2-X outperforms RT-2 by ~3× on emergent skills for the Google Robot, suggesting transfer from other robots adds new capabilities.
Including a short history of images and web-based pre-training are critical to achieving high performance for the large RT-2-X models.
